app.name: Copilot Cowork Estimator
app.tagline: Estimate Microsoft Copilot Cowork credits consumption
app.disclaimer: This tool produces indicative estimates. Values do not represent official or contractual Microsoft pricing.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.welcome: Welcome to the Copilot Cowork Estimator
dashboard.welcomeDesc: Create your first company to start estimating Copilot costs.

companies.title: Companies

companies.noScenarios: No scenarios for this company yet. Create the first scenario.

duplicateDialog.withScenariosDesc: Duplicate the company with all linked scenarios (new IDs assigned).

archiveDialog.title: Archive company
archiveDialog.body: The company will not be deleted. It will be excluded from dashboard totals and can be restored at any time.

restoreDialog.title: Restore company

deleteDialog.title: Delete company
deleteDialog.bodyNoScenarios: Are you sure you want to permanently delete this company?
deleteDialog.bodyWithScenarios: This company has {{count}} linked scenarios. Deleting it will also remove all associated scenarios. This action cannot be undone.
deleteDialog.typeToConfirm: Type the company name to confirm:

messages.archived: Company archived. It will not be included in dashboard totals.
messages.restored: Company restored and included in totals again.
messages.nameDuplicate: A company with this name already exists. You can continue or change the name.
messages.archivedNoEdit: This company is archived. Restore or duplicate it to edit.
messages.demoNoEdit: This is a demo company. Duplicate it to use it as a real scenario.

employees.segmentWarning: Sum of segments exceeds total employees

emptyState.title: No companies yet
emptyState.body: Create your first company to start building estimation scenarios.

segments.unclassifiedDesc: Employees not assigned to any segment
segments.overTotal: Sum of segments exceeds total employees

templates.title: Choose a template
templates.officeDesc: Executives, Sales, Finance, HR, IT, Operations
templates.manufacturingDesc: Executives, Office staff, Plant supervisors, Field Workers, Blue Collar
templates.serviceDesc: Management, Customer Care, Sales, Operations, Back Office

warnings.zeroHeadcount: This segment has zero people and generates no consumption.
warnings.excluded: This segment is excluded from the calculation.
warnings.highHeavy: This segment generates high heavy consumption.
warnings.customTaskMix: This segment uses a custom task mix.
warnings.nameEmpty: Enter a name for the segment.

profiles.title: Usage Profiles
profiles.deleteInUseError: This profile is used by one or more segments. Reassign them before deleting.
profiles.systemNotEditable: Standard profiles cannot be edited directly. Duplicate it to customize.
profiles.zeroTasksWarning: This profile generates no consumption (all tasks are zero).
profiles.highHeavyWarning: This profile assumes intensive use. Verify the assumption is realistic.

assumptions.title: Assumptions
assumptions.browserWarning: Warning: this task uses the browser, consumption may increase significantly.
assumptions.imageWarning: Warning: this task generates images, consumption depends on the image model.
assumptions.deletePresetError: This is a system preset and cannot be deleted.

modeDesc.payg: All estimated credits are valued at the price per credit. Most flexible mode.
modeDesc.prepaid: Advance purchase of credits at a discounted price. Prepaid credits are consumed before PAYG.
modeDesc.existing_capacity: Credits already available in the tenant. Consumed before generating additional costs.
modeDesc.blended: Combines existing capacity, prepaid credits and PAYG for the remainder.

settings.title: Settings
settings.resetConfirm: Are you sure? All local data will be permanently deleted.

errors.employeesRequired: Number of employees must be greater than 0

help.title: User Guide
help.subtitle: Everything you need to estimate Copilot Cowork credits.

intro.title: What this app is
intro.body: Copilot Cowork Estimator is a planning tool that estimates the consumption and cost of Copilot Credits for your organization. It runs entirely in the browser: no data is sent to external servers and everything is stored locally. Estimates are based on transparent, editable assumptions — not official Microsoft pricing.

companies.title: Managing companies
companies.body: Companies are the main container for all simulations. Create a company for each client or organization, then add linked estimation scenarios.
companies.createBody: Go to Companies → New Company. Name and number of employees are all you need to start. Industry, country, tags and notes are optional.
companies.archiveBody: Archive a company to exclude it from dashboard totals without deleting it. You can restore it at any time.
companies.deleteBody: Deletion requires confirmation. If the company has linked scenarios, you must type its name to confirm: all scenarios will be removed.
companies.duplicateBody: You can duplicate a company with just the profile data, or with all linked scenarios (new IDs are assigned).

segments.title: Workforce segmentation
segments.body: Segmentation divides the workforce into homogeneous groups to produce more realistic estimates. Each segment has a headcount, enablement percentages and a usage profile.
segments.addBody: Use predefined templates (Basic, Office, Manufacturing, Service) to start quickly, or add segments manually.
segments.calcBody: Every segment change immediately recalculates enabled users, active users, estimated credits and cost. The calculation uses the min/mid/max range.
segments.baselineBody: You can save the segmentation as a company baseline and copy it into new scenarios. Changes in a scenario do not alter the baseline.
segments.excludeBody: Use the 'Include in calculation' toggle to temporarily exclude a segment without deleting it. Useful for simulating partial rollouts.

wizard.title: Guided wizard for new scenarios
wizard.body: The wizard guides scenario creation through 7 progressive steps: you don't need to know all the advanced options to get a valid estimate.
wizard.stepsBody: Scenario (name + company) → Workforce → Usage profiles → Models & assumptions → Funding & budget → Review → Save & share.
wizard.draftBody: The wizard automatically saves a local draft. If you accidentally close the browser, the draft will be offered when you return.
wizard.reviewBody: The review shows min/mid/max credits and costs, per-segment breakdown and all warnings. You can go back to previous steps to fix issues.

profiles.title: Usage profiles
profiles.body: Usage profiles translate workforce segmentation into estimable monthly task volumes. Light, Medium and Heavy are the standard profiles; you can create custom ones.
profiles.standardBody: Light (occasional use), Medium (regular use) and Heavy (intensive use) are always available and cannot be deleted or directly edited.
profiles.customBody: Duplicate a standard profile to create your own variant. You can modify monthly tasks, the default model and advanced factors.
profiles.factorsBody: Factors (context, tools, runtime, browser, image) multiply the base consumption. The standard value is 1. Only modify them if you have specific needs.
profiles.impactBody: Each profile shows estimated credits and cost for a single active user, calculated with the default assumption pack.

taskPresets.title: Task presets & AI models
taskPresets.body: Task presets describe types of AI activities (deep research, document analysis, agent tasks...). Each preset has a min/mid/max credit band and complexity factors.
taskPresets.presetBody: Use system presets as a reference. You can duplicate and customize them. Presets using browser or image generation may have significantly higher consumption.
taskPresets.modelBody: Each model has a 'model factor' that multiplies the base consumption. Auto is the default model. You can enable/disable models and modify the factor (on editable models only).
taskPresets.packsBody: The assumption pack contains the credit bands (min/mid/max) for light/medium/heavy tasks and multiplier factors. Values are indicative estimates, not official pricing.

assumptionPacks.title: Versioned assumption packs
assumptionPacks.body: Each assumption pack is versioned and auditable. The system pack is the default. You can duplicate it to create custom versions to associate with your scenarios.
assumptionPacks.versionBody: Each pack has a name, version and source date. Scenarios save a reference to the pack used to ensure reproducibility of the calculation over time.
assumptionPacks.customBody: Duplicate the system pack to create your own variant. You can modify credit bands, factors and price per credit. Custom packs are exportable in the JSON backup.
assumptionPacks.deprecationBody: You can deprecate a custom pack to indicate it is obsolete. Deprecated packs are not suggested as default but remain available for historical scenarios.
assumptionPacks.compareBody: Use the ⇄ button on two packs to compare their credit bands and factors. Useful for evaluating the impact of a change to assumptions.

fundingCalculation.title: Funding plan & calculation engine
fundingCalculation.body: The funding plan transforms estimated credits into economic cost. The calculation engine produces min/mid/max credits from segments, profiles, models and assumption packs.
fundingCalculation.fundingBody: PAYG: all credits at full price. Prepaid: credits purchased in advance at a discounted price. Existing Capacity: credits already available in the tenant (no additional cost). Blended: combination.
fundingCalculation.engineBody: The formula is: credits = activeUsers × tasksPerMonth × creditsPerTask × modelFactor × contextFactor × toolsFactor × runtimeFactor. The result is always a min/mid/max range, never a single value.
fundingCalculation.budgetBody: You can set a monthly and annual budget. If the estimated cost exceeds the budget a warning is shown (max > budget) or a critical alert (even min > budget).

compareScenarios.title: Scenario comparison
compareScenarios.body: The Compare page lets you place two scenarios side-by-side on credits, costs, users and assumptions. See percentage deltas, side-by-side breakdown and assumption differences.
compareScenarios.metricsBody: Each row shows the value for A and B with a colored delta %: green = better value, red = worse. The color depends on the metric type (e.g. cost: lower is better).
compareScenarios.rangeBody: The range bar shows three values. The center dot indicates the midpoint, which is the main value. Min and max define the uncertainty interval.
compareScenarios.diffPackBody: If the two scenarios use different assumption packs, a warning appears. Differences in packs can explain part of the economic delta.

localStorage.title: Local storage
localStorage.body: All data stays in the browser (localStorage). It is never sent to a server. If you clear browser data, your simulations are lost too.
localStorage.backupBody: Export a JSON backup regularly from Settings. The file contains all companies, scenarios and custom configurations. You can re-import it on any browser.
localStorage.migrationBody: Saved data is automatically migrated to new app versions. The schema version number is visible in Settings.
localStorage.storageBody: The bar in Settings shows the space used. If it approaches the limit (~5 MB), export a backup and consider resetting less-used data.

exportImportShare.title: Export, Import & Sharing
exportImportShare.body: Export to CSV for analysis in Excel or Power BI. Export to JSON for backup and portability. Use the shareable link to send a scenario to a colleague without attachments.
exportImportShare.csvBody: From a scenario detail you can export a summary, segment breakdown or model breakdown. From the portfolio you can export all active scenarios in one file.
exportImportShare.jsonBody: The JSON backup includes companies, scenarios, custom profiles, custom packs and funding plans. Import it from Settings — the preview shows how many objects will be imported.
exportImportShare.shareBody: Click 'Share scenario' from a scenario detail. The link is generated in the browser (no server). The recipient sees a preview and can choose whether to save it in their browser.
exportImportShare.limitBody: For large scenarios the link may exceed the URL limit (~8KB). In that case the app automatically suggests using JSON export.

scenarioReport.title: Scenario report
scenarioReport.body: The report consolidates executive summary, company data, results, breakdown, funding, assumptions, formula, warnings and disclaimer in a printable and shareable view.
scenarioReport.printBody: Use the Print / PDF button to open the browser print dialog. From there you can save the report as PDF.
scenarioReport.sectionsBody: The report includes KPI, configuration, users, credits, costs, funding plan, budget, segment breakdown and the How this was calculated panel.
scenarioReport.disclaimerBody: The report is an estimate based on assumption packs. It does not represent official or binding Microsoft pricing.

valueInsights.title: Commercial value insights
valueInsights.body: Commercial insights are static deterministic texts that help read cost together with potential value: reduced repetitive work, faster processes and stakeholder talking points.
valueInsights.noAiBody: Texts are pre-built, versioned and translated. No external AI or backend call is made.
valueInsights.rulesBody: The engine selects insights based on industry, segments, usage profiles, cost and budget status.
valueInsights.commercialBody: Use them in the report to explain why an expensive scenario can still create value, while keeping caveats and disclaimers when budget is at risk.

quickEstimate.title: Quick estimate
quickEstimate.body: Quick estimate gives an order of magnitude with a few inputs, without completing the extended wizard.
quickEstimate.saveBody: After the preview you can save the result as a scenario linked to a new or existing company, then refine it later in the full wizard.
quickEstimate.scopeBody: Use it in early commercial conversations, when you need a fast answer on cost and credits before modeling all segments.
quickEstimate.benchmarkBody: The benchmark section shows an example by standard user profile with Light/Medium/Heavy prompts and illustrative 125/500/1,200 credits. It helps explain order of magnitude simply.

quickstart.title: Quick start in 4 steps
quickstart.step1Body: Go to Companies and create a new client with its name and number of employees.
quickstart.step2Body: Start a new scenario, pick the company and define the workforce segments (e.g. Sales, IT, Managers).
quickstart.step3Body: For each segment set headcount, enabled and active user percentages, usage profile and preferred AI model.
quickstart.step4Body: The scenario automatically computes monthly/annual credits and cost as a min/mid/max range, with a per-segment breakdown.

concepts.title: Key concepts
concepts.creditsBody: The consumption unit of the Cowork model. Each task consumes credits based on model, context, tools and complexity.
concepts.rangeBody: Estimates are not a single number but a range. The midpoint is the main value; min and max show the best and worst case.
concepts.profileBody: Light, Medium and Heavy describe how intensively a segment uses Copilot each month. You can also create custom ones.
concepts.assumptionBody: A versioned set of calculation rules (credit bands, model factors). Kept separate from company data and updatable.

dataPrivacy.title: Data privacy
dataPrivacy.body: All data stays in your browser's localStorage. Export a JSON backup regularly from Settings to avoid losing your work: clearing browser data also deletes your simulations.

faq.a1: No. They are planning estimates based on assumptions. They do not represent a Microsoft quote or contractual price.
faq.a2: Exclusively in the browser (localStorage). No server, no login, no tracking.
faq.a3: Yes, via JSON export or (coming soon) a shareable link. The recipient can import the configuration.
faq.q4: How do I update calculation assumptions?
faq.a4: In the Assumptions section you can review and duplicate assumption packs to create custom versions.

welcome.title: Welcome to Copilot Cowork Estimator
welcome.body: Let's walk through the app in a few steps. You can skip the tour anytime and restart it from the Help page.

nav.title: Navigation
nav.body: From here you reach Dashboard, Companies, Scenarios, Assumptions and Compare. On mobile use the menu at the top left.

companies.title: Manage companies
companies.body: Start by creating a company with its employee count. Each company can have multiple estimation scenarios.

companyNew.title: New company
companyNew.body: Click here to create a new company. Name and number of employees are all you need to start.

companiesFilters.title: Search and filter
companiesFilters.body: Search companies by name, sort by cost or headcount, and show/hide archived ones.

scenarios.title: Estimation scenarios
scenarios.body: A scenario models the workforce in segments and computes credits and costs as a range.

wizardHeader.title: Guided wizard
wizardHeader.body: The wizard guides you through 7 steps: scenario name, company, workforce, profiles, models, funding and review. You can save a draft at any time.

profiles.title: Usage profiles
profiles.body: Profiles (Light, Medium, Heavy) define how many monthly tasks an active user runs. You can duplicate them and create custom profiles.

profilesList.title: Profile cards
profilesList.body: Each card shows the task distribution and estimated cost per active user. Click 'Details' to see advanced factors.

assumptionsTabs.title: Assumptions section
assumptionsTabs.body: Here you find task presets, AI models and assumption packs. Values are indicative estimates — not official Microsoft pricing.

fundingEditor.title: Funding plan
fundingEditor.body: Configure how credits are valued: PAYG, prepaid, existing capacity or blended. The preview shows the monthly cost in real time.

breakdownSegments.title: Results breakdown
breakdownSegments.body: Here you see the breakdown by segment, model and intensity. The sum always matches the scenario total.

compareSelectors.title: Scenario comparison
compareSelectors.body: Select two scenarios from the menu. The table shows delta %, side-by-side breakdown and differences in the assumptions used.

settingsStorage.title: Local storage
settingsStorage.body: Here you see the space used, the number of saved items, the schema version and the last export. Export a JSON backup regularly.

shareBtn.title: Share scenario
shareBtn.body: Generate a shareable link to send this scenario to a colleague. The link contains all the data — no server involved.

reportOpen.title: Scenario report
reportOpen.body: Open a final printable, exportable and shareable scenario view, complete with assumptions and formula.

reportPrint.title: Print / PDF
reportPrint.body: This button opens browser print. You can save the report as PDF or send it to stakeholders.

quickEstimate.title: Quick estimate
quickEstimate.body: Here you get an immediate order of magnitude with a few fields. If the result is useful, save it as a scenario.

quickBenchmark.title: Standard profile benchmark
quickBenchmark.body: This table shows a commercial example with corporate, customer-facing, technical and manager profiles. It helps explain why heavy usage quickly increases cost.

valueInsights.title: Commercial value insights
valueInsights.body: This section interprets the result commercially with pre-built texts: value drivers, impacted activities, caveats and optimization suggestions.

tasksList.title: Task presets
tasksList.body: Each preset has a min/mid/max credit band and complexity indicators. Presets using browser or image generation may have higher consumption.

modelsList.title: AI models
modelsList.body: Enable or disable models, and modify the model factor for editable models. The factor multiplies the base task consumption.

segmentsAdd.title: Add segments
segmentsAdd.body: Create workforce segments: each segment has headcount, enablement percentages and a usage profile.

segmentsTable.title: Live calculation
segmentsTable.body: The table shows enabled and active users, credits and estimated cost per segment. Values update in real time.

theme.title: Theme and language
theme.body: Switch between light/dark theme and English or Italian at any time.

help.title: Need help?
help.body: The full guide is always here. Look for the info icons next to values for quick explanations.

restartStep.title: Restart the tour anytime
restartStep.body: This button restarts the guided tour at any time, from any page. Once you've seen it, the tour won't start on its own again.

hints.monthlyCredits: Sum of estimated monthly credits across all active scenarios, for the selected range (min/mid/max).
hints.monthlyCost: Estimated monthly cost, obtained by valuing the credits at the funding plan's price per credit.
hints.annualCost: Annual projection of the monthly cost (× 12). It is an estimate, not a spending commitment.
hints.warnings: Number of functional warnings across scenarios: custom assumptions, exceeded budgets, incomplete data, etc.
hints.rangeSelector: Change the displayed value: Min (optimistic), Mid (expected), Max (conservative).
hints.enabledPct: Percentage of people in the segment assigned a Copilot license.
hints.activePct: Percentage of enabled users who actually use Copilot on a recurring basis.
hints.usageProfile: Average usage intensity of the segment: defines how many light/medium/heavy tasks each user runs per month.
hints.preferredModel: AI model used by the segment. More powerful models consume more credits per task.
hints.assumptionPack: Versioned set of calculation rules applied to the scenario. Determines credit bands and factors.
hints.headcount: Total number of people in this workforce segment.
hints.modelFactor: Multiplier reflecting the higher credit consumption of more capable models.
hints.creditBands: Estimated credits per task by intensity (light/medium/heavy), in min/mid/max values.
hints.costPerActive: Average monthly cost per user actively using Copilot.
hints.costPerEnabled: Average monthly cost per user assigned a license.
hints.totalEmployees: Total number of employees in the organization. Used to compare segment coverage.
hints.estimatedKnowledgeWorkers: Estimated number of people who will actually use Cowork or are candidates for adoption. Useful for sizing the initial coverage.
hints.legalName: Official legal name, if different from the commercial name used in estimates.
hints.region: Geographic area or reference market, useful for segmenting multi-country portfolios.
hints.companyTags: Free labels to classify and filter the company in the portfolio.
hints.companyCurrency: Default currency for costing this company. Overrides the global default.
hints.companyMonthlyCost: Sum of estimated monthly costs (midpoint) across all active scenarios for this company.
hints.companyAnnualCost: Annual projection of the sum of monthly costs (× 12).
hints.pricePerCredit: Unit price per credit in the PAYG plan. Determines the final economic cost.
hints.existingCredits: Credits already available in the tenant. Consumed before PAYG credits.
hints.prepaidCredits: Credits purchased at a discounted price. Consumed after existing credits.
hints.discount: Percentage discount applied to the price per credit in the prepaid plan.
hints.monthlyBudget: Maximum monthly budget available for Copilot. Triggers an alert if the estimated cost exceeds it.

wizard.title: New Scenario
wizard.exitBody: You have unsaved changes. What do you want to do?
wizard.draftRecoveryBody: There is an autosaved draft from {{date}}. Do you want to resume it?
wizard.allSegmentsExcluded: All segments are excluded from the calculation. You can save as a draft or include at least one segment.
wizard.noSegments: Add at least one segment included in the calculation.

scenarioSetup.title: Create your scenario
scenarioSetup.noCompanies: No companies saved. Create the first one now.
scenarioSetup.nameDuplicate: A scenario with this name already exists.

workforce.title: Workforce segmentation
workforce.subtitle: Define the groups of users who will use Copilot.
workforce.useBaselineDesc: Load the segments saved in the company as a starting point.
workforce.useTemplateDesc: Choose a predefined template to start quickly.
workforce.addManualDesc: Create segments one at a time.

usageProfiles.title: Usage profiles
usageProfiles.subtitle: Review and customize the profile for each segment.

modelsAssumptions.title: Models & assumptions
modelsAssumptions.subtitle: Select the AI model and assumption pack for the calculation.
modelsAssumptions.officialGuideModelNote: In Official Guide Mode the model choice does not change the estimate: the Microsoft guide uses users × prompts × Light/Medium/Heavy ranges. Model factors such as 1.2×, 2×, etc. apply only in Advanced Driver-Adjusted or Custom Planning Mode.
modelsAssumptions.modelFactorSource: Model factors are relative planning assumptions, not official Microsoft pricing. In the Microsoft June 2026 pack they are neutralized to 1×.

fundingBudget.title: Funding plan & budget
fundingBudget.subtitle: Configure how credits are economically valued.

review.title: Scenario review
review.subtitle: Check results before saving.
review.canSaveReviewed: The scenario is complete and can be saved as Reviewed.
review.mustFixErrors: Fix blocking errors or save as a draft.

saveShare.title: Save & share
saveShare.subtitle: Save your scenario and choose how to share it.

compare.title: Scenario comparison
compare.subtitle: Select two scenarios to compare their credits, costs and assumptions.
compare.diffPackWarning: The scenarios use different assumption packs — the comparison may not be homogeneous.
compare.diffCompanyNote: Portfolio comparison: scenarios belong to different companies.
compare.noResults: One or both scenarios have no calculated result.

storage.title: Local storage
storage.backupSuggestion: Export a JSON backup regularly to avoid losing your data.

import.title: Import JSON
import.conflictWarning: Some IDs already exist. New IDs will be created.
import.importSuccess: Import completed. The dashboard has been updated.
import.importError: Invalid file. No data has been modified.
import.schemaFuture: The file was created with a newer version of the app.

share.title: Share scenario
share.payloadTooLong: The payload exceeds the URL limit. Use JSON export to share.
share.payloadWarning: The link is long. It may not work in all messaging systems.
share.disclaimerNote: The link contains all scenario data. It does not include sensitive information.
share.payloadCorrupted: The link is not valid or is corrupted.
share.payloadTooLargeUse: The link is too large. Ask the sender to export as JSON.

insights.title: Commercial value insights
insights.subtitle: Pre-built deterministic text: no AI runtime and no data leaves the browser.

default.title: Executive reading
default.body: This scenario helps turn Copilot adoption into an operational-capacity discussion: which groups use the technology, how intensively and with what estimated economic impact. Read the value together with productivity, reduction of repetitive work and quality of output.

highCost.title: High cost: a value narrative is needed
highCost.body: The estimated cost is material. To support it commercially, connect the spend to high-value use cases: reduced time on recurring work, increased analytical capacity, faster document review and support for operational decisions.

manufacturing.title: Prudent rollout in manufacturing
manufacturing.body: In manufacturing contexts, value usually appears first in office roles, supervisors and digitally enabled operations. Avoid broad rollouts to populations with low expected usage: start where reporting, documents and coordination matter most.

finance.title: Finance: control, analysis and reporting
finance.body: For Finance, Copilot can reduce time spent preparing reports, reconciling documents, summarizing variances and doing preliminary analysis. The strongest narrative is speed and traceability of analytical work, not only operational cost reduction.

professionalServices.title: Professional services: more scalable delivery
professionalServices.body: In professional services, value can come from knowledge reuse, faster deliverables, meeting summaries and client-document preparation. Position the scenario as a lever to increase delivery capacity without proportional manual effort.

sales.title: Sales: less time on research and preparation
sales.body: For Sales teams, the main value can come from reducing time spent on customer research, account planning, meeting summaries and proposal personalization. Position it as a productivity accelerator for high-volume pre-sales work.

legal.title: Legal: faster document review
legal.body: For Legal, intensive usage can be justified when the goal is faster document review, clause comparison, contract summaries and preliminary due diligence. The value is more analytical capacity and traceability, not replacing legal judgment.

hr.title: HR: policies, communications and internal materials
hr.body: For HR, value can emerge from policy summarization, communication drafting, document analysis and employee-material preparation. It is a good candidate for Medium profiles with recurring but not necessarily heavy activity.

it.title: IT: knowledge base, tickets and automation
it.body: For IT, value can concentrate on ticket summaries, technical documentation, knowledge bases, troubleshooting and automation of repetitive work. In heavy scenarios, clarify which workflows are truly high-value.

customerCare.title: Customer care: faster, more consistent responses
customerCare.body: In customer care, Copilot can help retrieve knowledge, summarize cases, prepare responses and reduce repetitive handling time. Value is higher when the knowledge base is structured and up to date.

whiteCollar.title: White collar: reducing friction in daily work
whiteCollar.body: For white-collar users, value often comes from recurring micro-tasks: summaries, email, reports, internal search and document rework. Even small individual savings can become material across large populations.

heavy.title: Heavy usage: position on high-value cases
heavy.body: Heavy segments indicate complex or frequent work. To justify the cost, connect these users to measurable use cases: deep research, multi-step workflows, document analysis or automations that free qualified capacity.

costWhy.title: Why it costs this much: not a license, operational capacity
costWhy.body: The strongest commercial framing is to separate the price from a simple software-license view. You are not only buying access to a tool: you are funding digital work capacity on activities that currently consume qualified hours, such as research, summarization, document analysis, report preparation and repetitive workflows. The cost should therefore be explained as additional operational capacity, not as an isolated IT expense.

financeObjection.title: How to answer the Finance objection
financeObjection.body: If Finance challenges the cost, do not defend it as technology spend. Move the conversation to scope, active population and covered activities: which segments will actually use Copilot, how many repetitive activities are absorbed and which processes can be accelerated. Then propose a controlled rollout with adoption measurement and budget guardrails.

valueFrame.title: Sales frame: time released and work quality
valueFrame.body: The narrative to use is: Copilot can free time from low-perceived-value but high-effort activities — preliminary research, summaries, rewriting, data gathering, document preparation — allowing teams to focus on decisions, customer relationships, quality control and execution.

rolloutClose.title: Close the proposal: start where value is obvious
rolloutClose.body: To make an expensive scenario sustainable, propose a first phase on segments with clear and measurable value. Sales, Legal, IT, Finance or Customer Care often allow quick proof of reduced repetitive work and faster execution. Only after validation should the rollout expand.

budgetWarning.title: Budget at risk on maximum value
budgetWarning.body: The maximum value exceeds the configured budget. Before presenting the scenario, consider phased rollout, reducing heavy segments, using Auto models or reviewing the most expensive task assumptions.

budgetCritical.title: Budget exceeded even in minimum case
budgetCritical.body: The minimum cost exceeds budget: the scope needs review. Reduce active users, postpone lower-priority segments or evaluate capacity/prepaid before positioning the scenario as sustainable.

customAssumptions.title: Custom assumptions to explain
customAssumptions.body: The scenario uses custom task mix or factors. In conversations with Finance or customers, explain why these values were chosen and use the calculation panel as the auditable reference.

default.title: How to optimize the proposal
default.body: If the cost looks high, move the conversation to phased rollout: start with segments where value is clearest, measure real adoption and expand progressively. Avoid enabling low-usage populations in the first phase.

report.title: Scenario report
report.partialResult: Partial report: this scenario has no calculated result yet.

quick.title: Quick estimate
quick.subtitle: Get an order of magnitude with a few inputs. You can save it as a scenario and refine it in the full wizard.
quick.preliminary: Preliminary estimate: use the full wizard to model segments and advanced assumptions.

quickBenchmark.title: Monthly estimate by standard user profile
quickBenchmark.subtitle: Quick example using credits per prompt: Light 125, Medium 500, Heavy 1,200. Useful to explain order of magnitude before saving a scenario.
quickBenchmark.howToReadBody: this table does not replace the official scenario calculation. It is a commercial benchmark: it shows how cost changes when a person runs more light, medium or heavy prompts. For a saveable estimate use the form above and save as scenario.
